root_text.an4.8:1.1: “Cattārimāni, bhikkhave, tathāgatassa vesārajjāni, yehi vesārajjehi samannāgato tathāgato āsabhaṁ ṭhānaṁ paṭijānāti, parisāsu sīhanādaṁ nadati, brahmacakkaṁ pavatteti.
root_text.an4.8:1.3: ‘“Sammāsambuddhassa te paṭijānato ime dhammā anabhisambuddhā”ti tatra vata maṁ samaṇo vā brāhmaṇo vā devo vā māro vā brahmā vā koci vā lokasmiṁ sahadhammena paṭicodessatī’ti nimittametaṁ, bhikkhave, na samanupassāmi.
root_text.an4.8:1.4: Etamahaṁ, bhikkhave, nimittaṁ asamanupassanto khemappatto abhayappatto vesārajjappatto viharāmi.
root_text.an4.8:2.1: ‘“Khīṇāsavassa te paṭijānato ime āsavā aparikkhīṇā”ti tatra vata maṁ samaṇo vā brāhmaṇo vā devo vā māro vā brahmā vā koci vā lokasmiṁ sahadhammena paṭicodessatī’ti nimittametaṁ, bhikkhave, na samanupassāmi.
root_text.an4.8:2.2: Etamahaṁ, bhikkhave, nimittaṁ asamanupassanto khemappatto abhayappatto vesārajjappatto viharāmi.
root_text.an4.8:3.1: ‘“Ye kho pana te antarāyikā dhammā vuttā te paṭisevato nālaṁ antarāyāyā”ti tatra vata maṁ samaṇo vā brāhmaṇo vā devo vā māro vā brahmā vā koci vā lokasmiṁ sahadhammena paṭicodessatī’ti nimittametaṁ, bhikkhave, na samanupassāmi.
root_text.an4.8:3.2: Etamahaṁ, bhikkhave, nimittaṁ asamanupassanto khemappatto abhayappatto vesārajjappatto viharāmi.
root_text.an4.8:4.1: ‘“Yassa kho pana te atthāya dhammo desito so na niyyāti takkarassa sammā dukkhakkhayāyā”ti tatra vata maṁ samaṇo vā brāhmaṇo vā devo vā māro vā brahmā vā koci vā lokasmiṁ sahadhammena paṭicodessatī’ti nimittametaṁ, bhikkhave, na samanupassāmi.
root_text.an4.8:4.2: Etamahaṁ, bhikkhave, nimittaṁ asamanupassanto khemappatto abhayappatto vesārajjappatto viharāmi.
root_text.an4.8:4.3: Imāni kho, bhikkhave, cattāri tathāgatassa vesārajjāni, yehi vesārajjehi samannāgato tathāgato āsabhaṁ ṭhānaṁ paṭijānāti, parisāsu sīhanādaṁ nadati, brahmacakkaṁ pavattetīti.

translation_text.an4.8:1.1: “Mendicants, a Realized One has four kinds of self-assurance. With these he claims the bull’s place, roars his lion’s roar in the assemblies, and turns the divine wheel.
translation_text.an4.8:1.3: I see no reason for anyone—whether ascetic, brahmin, god, Māra, or the Divinity, or anyone else in the world—to legitimately scold me, saying: ‘You claim to be a fully awakened Buddha, but you don’t understand these things.’
translation_text.an4.8:1.4: Since I see no such reason, I live secure, fearless, and assured.
translation_text.an4.8:2.1: I see no reason for anyone—whether ascetic, brahmin, god, Māra, or the Divinity, or anyone else in the world—to legitimately scold me, saying: ‘You claim to have ended all defilements, but you still have these defilements.’
translation_text.an4.8:2.2: Since I see no such reason, I live secure, fearless, and assured.
translation_text.an4.8:3.1: I see no reason for anyone—whether ascetic, brahmin, god, Māra, or the Divinity, or anyone else in the world—to legitimately scold me, saying: ‘The acts that you say are obstructions are not really obstructions for the one who performs them.’
translation_text.an4.8:3.2: Since I see no such reason, I live secure, fearless, and assured.
translation_text.an4.8:4.1: I see no reason for anyone—whether ascetic, brahmin, god, Māra, or the Divinity, or anyone else in the world—to legitimately scold me, saying: ‘Though you teach that this teaching leads to the goal of the complete ending of suffering, it doesn’t lead there for one who practices it.’
translation_text.an4.8:4.2: Since I see no such reason, I live secure, fearless, and assured.
translation_text.an4.8:4.3: A Realized One possesses these four kinds of self-assurance. With these he claims the bull’s place, roars his lion’s roar in the assemblies, and turns the divine wheel.
translation_text.an4.8:5.2: that ascetics and brahmins rely on
translation_text.an4.8:5.3: vanish on reaching a Realized One,
translation_text.an4.8:5.4: assured, gone beyond grounds for criticism.
translation_text.an4.8:6.1: He rolls forth the Wheel of Dhamma <j>as a consummate one,
translation_text.an4.8:6.2: complete, sympathetic for all living creatures.
translation_text.an4.8:6.3: Sentient beings revere him, <j>first among gods and humans,

reference_text.an4.8:1.1: cck21.11, csp1ed15.315, csp2ed15.315, dr21.11, ms15A4_48, msdiv8, pts-vp-pli2.9, sya21.11, vri36.10
